channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This money-saving emporium is like Facebook Marketplace for gift cards
item.description: You don’t have to be an avid reader of restaurant industry trade publications—though I can attest that they are oddly fascinating—to realize that everything’s getting more expensive. The good news is that there’s an easy way to counteract those rising menu prices.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Your latest prescription is to get outside
item.description: Health care providers are trying a new tactic to promote the healing effects of nature: They're actually writing prescriptions for it.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: If the new Prime Video movie Hedda reminds you of Saltburn, you’re not the only one – but the cast don’t see it
item.description: Is the new movie Hedda really the next Saltburn? The cast ‘don’t see it,’ but you still can stream both on Prime Video.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Valentine, TechRadar

item.title: Here are 20-plus kid tools for higher-quality screen time
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Not everything creative needs a prompt. The web is increasingly flooded with AI-generated images and videos, much of it aimed at kids.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Sora is even fooling human deepfake detectors
item.description: It used to be that artificial intelligence would leave behind helpful clues that an image it produced was not, in fact, real. Previous generations of the technology might give a person an extra finger or even an additional limb.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: Google TPUs find sweet spot of AI demand, a decade after chip’s debut
item.description: In an AI chip industry that's almost entirely commanded by Nvidia Corp., a Google chip first developed more than 10 years ago especially for artificial intelligence tasks is finally gaining momentum outside its home company as a way to train and run complex AI models.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Age verification could soon land in Russia – putting already precarious internet freedoms on the line
item.description: Russians could soon lose their anonymity on the internet, required to upload ther passport to access a wealth of online content.
item.dc:creator: Chiara Castro, TechRadar

item.title: Trump 'terribly advised' on crypto billionaire pardon: Key supporter
item.description: Venture capitalist Joe Lonsdale, a key supporter of President Trump, suggested Thursday that the president was “terribly advised” on his decision to pardon Binance founder Changpeng Zhao.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: In a California farming region, researchers are mapping rural heat to protect farmworkers
item.description: In the summers, the sky is jet black when Raul Cruz arrives at this Imperial Valley sugarcane field to start his day. He chops, cleans and bundles the crop, taking heed as the sun rises. It's hard work, but so is starting at 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and JAE C. HONG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to watch Mexico City Grand Prix 2025 F1: free streams, preview, schedule, Verstappen quickest in practice as Norris and Piastri toil
item.description: All the ways to watch Mexico City Grand Prix live streams online and from anywhere, as Max Verstappen nears Oscar Piastri and Lando Norris in the title race.
item.dc:creator: Andy Murray, TechRadar

item.title: 5 big updates coming to the ChatGPT Atlas browser
item.description: OpenAI is rushing out quality-of-life upgrades for ChatGPT Atlas that promise to make the AI-powered browser better.
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: From friendly text to financial trap: the new scam trend
item.description: Friendly text conversations about BBQs and social events can lead to WEEX gold trading scams that target older adults with fake investment opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: Elon Musk literally left a government job with a black eye, has seen profits at his car company sink and must worry about federal regulators disrupting his plans to unleash a fleet of driverless taxis on America's roads in the next few years.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump calls off plans to deploy federal troops to San Francisco
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he would not deploy federal troops to San Francisco, reversing course on plans to implement anticrime and immigration enforcement in the Bay Area.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, The Hill

item.title: Celtic vs Sturm Graz live stream – How to watch Europa League online, FREE trial, kick-off time
item.description: Celtic take on Sturm Graz: European night sparks, Austrian counter-press chaos, and Kyogo Furuhashi magic — who will roar the loudest in the Europa League 2025?
item.dc:creator: Tom Bailey, TechRadar

item.title: A practitioner’s primer on deterministic application modernization
item.description: Large organizations rarely have just a handful of applications. They have thousands, often representing billions of lines of code. These code bases span decades of frameworks, libraries, and shifting best practices.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: American e-waste is causing a 'hidden tsunami' in Southeast Asia, report says
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Millions of tons of discarded electronics from the United States are being shipped overseas, much of it to developing countries in Southeast Asia unprepared to safely handle hazardous waste, according to a new report released Wednesday by an environmental watchdog.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reddit sues Perplexity and others for allegedly scraping millions of user comments
item.description: Social media platform Reddit sued the artificial intelligence company Perplexity AI and three other entities on Wednesday, alleging their involvement in an “industrial-scale, unlawful” economy to “scrape” the comments of millions of Reddit users for commercial gain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: 85% of developers use AI regularly – JetBrains survey
item.description: AI usage has become standard practice in software development, with 85% of developers in a recent JetBrains survey citing regular use of AI tools for coding and development. Additionally, 62% were relying on at least one AI-powered coding assistant, agent, or code editor.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reddit sues AI company Perplexity and others for 'industrial-scale' scraping of user comments
item.description: Social media platform Reddit sued the artificial intelligence company Perplexity AI and three other entities on Wednesday, alleging their involvement in an “industrial-scale, unlawful” economy to “scrape” the comments of millions of Reddit users for commercial gain.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Kevin O’Leary says ‘Marty Supreme’ blew millions by not using AI actors
item.description: The “Shark Tank” star said the production costs are outdated when AI can create realistic looking background characters.
item.dc:creator: Teddy Grant, NewsNation

item.title: Meghan Markle, Prince Harry join call for AI 'superintelligence' ban
item.description: The couple is among more than 700 public figures to sign on to a brief statement by the Future of Life Institute.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Meta cutting 600 AI jobs even as it continues to hire more for its superintelligence lab
item.description: MENLO PARK, Calif. (AP) — Meta Platforms is cutting roughly 600 artificial intelligence jobs even as it continues to hire more workers for its superintelligence lab, the company confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ukraine unveils upgraded sea drone it says can strike anywhere in the Black Sea
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's state security service has unveiled an upgraded sea drone it says can now operate anywhere in the Black Sea, carry heavier weapons and use artificial intelligence for targeting.
item.dc:creator: EFREM LUKATSKY and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Google and Apple face extra UK scrutiny over 'strategic' role in mobile platforms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain’s antitrust watchdog on Wednesday targeted Google and Apple for their “strategic” roles in mobile ecosystems, opening the door for regulators to impose changes to their business practices to improve competition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Florida test scores rise, attendance improves after cellphone ban
item.description: A new study finds that Florida test scores rose and attendance improved after a school cellphone ban, although short-term disciplinary problems also arose in the state.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Netflix blames tax dispute in Brazil for rare quarterly earnings letdown
item.description: Netflix missed the earnings target set by stock market analysts during the video streamer’s latest quarter, a letdown that the company blamed on a tax dispute in Brazil. The results announced Tuesday broke Netflix's six-quarter streak of posting a profit that eclipsed analysts' projections.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Forget blueprints. AI is learning to sketch buildings from scratch, starting with just your words
item.description: "AI will play a significant role in the architectural design process, starting from a supporting tool and eventually becoming a co-designer," Wei Yan said. The post Forget blueprints.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal News Network’s Industry Exchange Cloud 2025
item.description: Unlock the future of cloud innovation The post Federal News Network’s Industry Exchange Cloud 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: The lost 30 years: The decline of Japan’s semiconductor industry
item.description: Global industrial competition has entered an era in which “whoever controls semiconductors controls the future.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to improve technical documentation with generative AI
item.description: Devops teams have a love-hate relationship with writing and consuming technical documentation. Developers loathe reading and maintaining undocumented code. Architecture diagrams can tell a great story, but much of it is fiction compared to the implemented architecture.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Amazon cloud outage resolved after downing dozens of major websites, apps
item.description: Amazon Web Services provides infrastructure for many big-name companies, as well as state and local governments.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Safety app Citizen to pay users to go live on the platform
item.description: The company posted a social media update, alerting users that they could now be paid for live-streaming incidents that occur around them.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, NewsNation
